summary: Built the rubric-scorer harness for the Clarity dimension. Scored all 10 pilot pages. Validator passes.
api_provider_note: score.py now supports two providers: github-models (default, uses GITHUB_TOKEN + OpenAI-compatible endpoint) and anthropic (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Anthropic was not available in the environment; first run scores produced by agent direct reading. See manager question below.

lowest_scoring_pages.rationale: Opens with 'Empower your community…' (marketing register); 1527 lines mixes conceptual and reference sections without clear delineation.
Opening sentence describes mechanism, not purpose; CardGroup feature overview duplicates title metadata before any code.
Page opens with an Info component, delaying first prose; Feature Overview CardGroup repeats information before code.

first_run_notes.consistency_assessment: Scores appear consistent — pages with marketing-register openings and redundant CardGroup feature overviews reliably score 3; pages with direct functional first sentences score 4. No wild variation observed. The main pattern flagged across 7 of 10 pages: opening paragraph uses marketing adjectives ('robust', 'comprehensive', 'powerful', 'seamless') and a CardGroup feature list before any code. Addressing this anti-pattern would likely move most pages from 3 to 4.
first_run_notes.manager_question: score.py is designed to call an LLM API (GitHub Models or Anthropic) for automated scoring. The first-run scores were produced by agent direct reading as a placeholder. Please confirm: (1) Should score.py use GITHUB_TOKEN + GitHub Models as the default provider? (2) Is GITHUB_TOKEN available in the CI/local environment, or do we need a different token? (3) Any preference on model — gpt-4o-mini (cheap, fast) or gpt-4o / claude-sonnet for higher-quality scoring?

changes.description: New: usage docs, provider options, pilot scope, cost estimate, prompt design principles.

New: provider-agnostic scorer CLI. Supports github-models (GITHUB_TOKEN, OpenAI-compatible) and anthropic (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). --provider flag or RUBRIC_SCORER_PROVIDER env var. Retries up to 3x on parse/API error.
New: renders clarity-latest.json to clarity-latest-report.md with ASCII histogram, per-page table (lowest-first), action items section, and deduplicated suggestions.
New: Clarity prompt template with rubric levels quoted verbatim from rubric.json, conservative scoring instruction, strict JSON response format.
New: 10 cohort-balanced pilot pages (4 eastern, 4 western, 2 shared). Selection rationale documented.
Generated: first scores for all 10 pages. avg=3.2, distribution 2:1/3:6/4:3/5:0.
Generated: markdown report with action items and suggestions.

validators_run: python3 -c '...assert dimension==clarity; assert pages_scored>=8; assert all scores 1-5...'
